surface.name: Groq API Quickstart, Python chat-completion route
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, the Python SDK tab in the general Quickstart is the fastest commonly used documented route: it provides one install command and a complete chat-completion program. Signup is one opaque step at the official login URL; documented key, environment, install, and request steps follow.

alternatives_considered.name: cURL
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer language tab; Python is the fully visible SDK route on the inspected page.

alternatives_considered.name: JavaScript
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer language tab rather than a separately recommended getting-started surface.

alternatives_considered.name: Playground
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Quickstart lists Playground after the first chat completion as a next step.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a GroqCloud account and API key, then run the official Python first chat completion.
assumed_prior_state: No GroqCloud account, organization, project, API key, SDK, or request is assumed.
Python and pip are available locally; no package installation or API request was executed during research.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The published Python program sends a request to Groq and prints the returned chat-completion content.
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Quickstart explicitly identifies the received chat completion as success and places Playground and other endpoints in Next Steps.

prerequisites.requirement: A GroqCloud Account is required to use the Cloud Services.
Only a team owner or a user with the developer role may create or manage API keys.
A Groq API key is required by the Quickstart request.
Python and pip are needed for the visible Python SDK route.

primary_path.output: The ordered Create an API Key, key setup, and first chat completion sections are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The Create API Key link is available.

primary_path.action: Sign up for or sign in to GroqCloud.
details: Treat signup as one opaque workshop-policy step at the official URL.
Continuing accepts the Services Agreement and acknowledges the Privacy Policy.
primary_path.success_signal: The API Keys page is accessible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Follow the displayed authentication recovery path.

primary_path.action: Create and copy an API key.
details: Open API Keys and select `Create API Key`.
Use an owner or developer-role identity.
primary_path.success_signal: The new key value is available to copy.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Ask a team owner for the developer role if key creation is unavailable.

primary_path.output: Credential available to the Python process.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Correct shell quoting or environment configuration.
Resolve Python environment or package errors.

primary_path.action: Run the published first chat-completion program.
details: Send the prompt `Explain the importance of fast language models` and print the first response message.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Check the API key, model identifier, account limits, and network response.

branches.condition: Existing authenticated GroqCloud account and project
branches.path: The user may already have console access and generate a project-specific key, but the current public key-generation UI sequence remains undocumented.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Could bypass signup while leaving the required key form and one-time-secret behavior unestablished.

friction_gates.description: The key link requires an Account and authenticated GroqCloud console state.
The public API Keys shell exposes the action but not the authenticated key form or secret-display sequence.
The public Login shell says continuing accepts the Groq Services Agreement and acknowledges the Privacy Policy.

time_to_first_success.value: The Quickstart says the Groq API can be running in a few minutes; the blocked public route prevents an end-to-end verification of that claim.

sources.title: API Keys
evidence_supported: Authenticated key form and secret transition are not publicly exposed

sources.title: Projects
evidence_supported: Project creation and project-specific API keys after console access

sources.title: GroqCloud
evidence_supported: Public signup and authentication sequence remains unexposed

excluded_after_success: production rate limits and billing upgrades
